segment-text: Written by
and Patrick McHale
"Pinocchio" by C. Collodi
EXT. THE HILL - DUSK
Now this was quite a few years before my time, but I learned the story... and then it became my story...

Some autumn leaves are blowing by. Small hill by a pine forest, and RED birch trees. AUTUMN. A lonely figure standing by a wooden grave marker. This is GEPPETTO. Able-bodied and in his mid-seventies, but sorrow ages him. A shock of white hair- matching his unruly beardhaloing his strong, noble face. He is consumed by grief. He kneels by the grave, brushes off the snow. Embedded- and framed- by the wooden grave marker, there is a photograph of a beautiful boy of TEN, Carlo. And the inscription on the grave reads: CARLO, MY ANGEL, Gone back to heaven, February, 1916. CRICKET (V.O.)
Geppetto looks at the photograph and smiles a tremulous smile. A tear rolls down his cheek. CAMERA pushes on the photograph. We transition to:
EXT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP - TREE (THE PAST) - DAY
Cold WINTER day. CARLO swings in a tree swing. Looks up at planes passing overhead. Jumps off the swing. He rushes back to the house, pretending to be an airplane.
(airplane sounds)
INT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP - DAY
The workshop is a WARM wonderland of toys and marionettes. Everything is hand-carved: doors, furniture, shelves, utensils.
Papa, Papa... Yes?
Guess what I saw! What? Guess!
I have no idea.
I saw some planes!
Oh, did you? Good.
Geppetto is carving a puppet. Carlo tries to steal a look.
What is it you're making this time, Papa? Guess.
A soldier? A magician? A witch?
No, no, no, no, no, no, no! You'll have to wait and see, Carlo. All good things require patience!
-DRAG WOOD BACK TO THE HOUSE. -FEED THE FIRE TOGETHER.
They wanted for nothing; all they needed was each other's company.
-HAVE A MODEST BUT ABUNDANT MEAL TOGETHER. CROSS THEMSELVES AND EAT.
(quietly praying)
INT. GEPPETTO BEDROOM - NIGHT
Carlo is in his hand-carved bed, decorated with Italian fairytale pictures. Light snow falls outside a window. Geppetto reads Carlo a fairy tale.
And the old witch warned the little hedgehog- do not tell lies, or your nose will grow and grow- up to here!
Geppetto extends his finger out from his nose to demonstrate it growing. Carlo's eyes sparkle, still immersed in the story.
His nose would grow?
Lies, my dear boy, are found out immediately, because they are like long noses- visible to all but the teller of the lie... (beat) And the more you lie... the more it grows...
They both laugh as Geppetto encompasses Carlo in the blanket, tickling him through the fabric.
(yawns) Sing me Mama's song so I can sleep, please, Papa?
Geppetto grabs his accordion and starts playing a sweet LULLABY. He clears his throat and then begins.
(singing) My Son... My son... You are my shining Sun. My moon. My stars. My clear blue daylight sky.
Carlo closes his eyes and what follows is a series of vignettes over different seasons. -GEPPETTO PUSHES CARLO ON A SWING.
Higher, Papa! Higher!
And if you looked at me today, my heart would heal so fast. And if you held me right away, I'd be complete at last... At last...
-GEPPETTO TEACHES CARLO TO CARVE MARIONETTES.
EXT. BUTCHER'S SHOP
Geppetto installs a pig-doorbell on the BUTCHER'S SHOP. Try it.
Carlo pulls the strings to test it out.
Thank the ladies.
The BUTCHER SISTERS laugh at how excited Carlo is. How cute. Geppetto walks Carlo up to the school and gives him a book. For you.
(small gasp) For me?
For you, Carlo.
Waves goodbye to Carlo as he heads off to SCHOOL. Carlo joins
INT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP
Carlo studies his SCHOOLBOOK beside his father. Geppetto proudly glances at his studious son. Geppetto carves the FACE OF A CHRIST.
INT. CHURCH - DUSK
Geppetto and Carlo install the unpainted crucifix in church.
Pull, Carlo! Pull!
Magnificent work, Geppetto. Thank you.
EXT. THE HILL - DAY
Geppetto rests in a bed of moss near the forest, a picnic spread out before him. Carlo flies a kite nearby, giggling with childish glee as it dances upon the wind.
My Son, My Son: You are my golden Sun.
-GEPPETTO AND CARLO CHOP DOWN A TREE. As it starts to fall Geppetto lunges forward to quickly pull Carlo out of the way.
(stuttering) Watch out, Carlo!
-CARLO LOOKS FOR A PERFECT PINE CONE.
(speaking) When one life is lost, another must grow.
How about this one?
Carlo shows him a scraggly-looking pine cone.
(speaking) No- no- no- no, Carlo. It has to be perfect- complete. See? This one's missing some of its scales...
Carlo nods, examining the imperfect pine cone. -GEPPETTO CARVES. CARLO WATCHES AND HELPS.
My Son, My Son: You are my shining Sun...
INT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP - DUSK
Geppetto carves a pair of clogs for Carlo. Then PAINTS them beautifully in bright colors.
INT. GEPPETTO BEDROOM
Carlo yawns and sleepily nestles back down in his pillow. Geppetto puts down his accordion and gets into his own bed.
Good night, Papa...
Good night, my son.
EXT. TOWN STREETS - DAY WINTER -- February, 1916

Snow still lingers around town. Geppetto and Carlo go by, dragging a WOODEN CART filled with the paints and tools. Geppetto is well groomed. Carlo sits on the back of the cart, proudly wearing his new brightly colored clogs.
I love my new shoes, Papa.
I'm very glad, Carlo.
We're going to church first, right?
Oh yes, yes.
Carlo spots a stray dog on the side of the road.
Hello there, little dog.
We see the town: a BEAUTIFUL village with turn-of-the-century buildings peppered amongst Medieval hamlets and workshops:
The spirit of the town is LIVELY. Geppetto and Carlo wave to people as they travel through town.
STREET SWEEP WOMAN
Buongiorno, Geppetto. Carlo and Geppetto pass by an alley with sleeping dogs and lighthearted posters. They pass by more townspeople going about their day.
Morning!
CARNIVAL WORKER #2
THE BUTCHER says "hello" as do the YOUNG TWIN DAUGHTERS.
Master Geppetto! You gonna finish that crucifix today?
We're gonna do our best! Ladies.
Such a perfectionist!
Such a perfectionist!
The BLACKSMITH and his WIFE hammer horseshoes.
(he will become PODESTA) A model Italian citizen!
And such a good father.
Carlo, nice shoes. Catch!
The FRUIT VENDOR tosses him an orange.
Thank you, sir!
Good morning. Hi!

Geppetto and Carlo head up towards a MASSIVE STONE CHURCH, romanesque and looming above the town, casting a shadow over it. A few people bustle about in front. THE DOCTOR AND HIS WIFE pass by and greet them.
Buongiorno.
Buongiorno, Dottore.
THE TOWN PRIEST awaits Geppetto on the steps.
Ahh, Carlo. Geppetto.
The Priest reaches out and shakes Geppetto's hand to welcome
Carlo runs ahead and sees the nearly finished Crucifix.
INT. CHURCH - EVENING
Geppetto still painting. Carlo looks up from his SCHOOL BOOK.
It looks great, Papa! Is it almost time to go home?
Almost. Send up a little more red for me.
CARLO looks in Geppetto's COMPLEX TOOL/PAINT BOX and passes a paint jar, using a bucket.
Oh! I forgot to show you what I found!
What is it, my boy??
You'll see...Ta-da!!!
Carlo has hidden a PINE CONE in the bucket.
(laughing) The PERFECT pine cone! It still has all its scales!
I thought I could plant it myself and watch the tree grow. And then carve toys for myself- like you do.
Geppetto laughs, proud.
I think that's as good an idea as any boy ever had, Carlo...
Geppetto tosses the pine cone down to Carlo. He fumbles and bats it around several times before safely securing it.
A low rumble drones above their heads.
What's that sound, Papa?
Geppetto listens.
Is it a plane?
Geppetto grows concerned, but shakes it off. He climbs down the scaffold- agile- nimble.
Gather the tools- quickly. We'll go home to a warm fire and hot soup.
Can we have some hot chocolate too?
(distracted) Sure, sure.
It seems like a hot chocolate sort of day...doesn't it?
Oh yes, yes, yes. Fa-fine.
Geppetto isn't listening. His attention is on the ominous sounds outside. He heads towards the exit of the church. Carlo starts to follow him.
EXT. CHURCH - NIGHT
Geppetto stands at the foot of the church and looks up in the sky.
Papa, what is it?
Nothing. I'm sure it's nothing, but...
Oh wait, my pine cone!
Carlo returns to collect his pine cone. The sky is peppered with planes, going to war. Bombs explode in the distance.
It was later said that Geppetto's little town was not even a targetthat these planes were heading back to base and simply let go of their bombs to make their ballast lighter...
One of the planes- THE BIGGEST ONE-Releases 3 large bombs. THE BOMBS FALL- whistling-heading for the groundGEPPETTO (gasps)

Geppetto runs back to the churchInside the church Carlo finds his missing pine cone. -But it is too late! The bombs hit the church and burn the frame. Debris flies everywhere. Geppetto falls to the ground. His ears ringing. All he can see is fire. Something lands near him: Carlo's perfect PINE CONE. No. Carlo.
GEPPETTO (CONT'D)
Geppetto passes out. CAMERA TRACKS INTO: THE BURNING CHURCH.
EXT. THE HILL - DUSK

Geppetto plants the pine cone by Carlo's grave, and cries. A few MOURNERS keep their distance. Then they move away, leaving Geppetto alone with his grief. Two men hammer the headstone into the ground.
INT. CHURCH
Geppetto never left his side- and that was that. He worked very little, he ate even less... (MORE) CRICKET (V.O.) (CONT'D) and the Church's crucifix remained unfinished...
-The bombed out church- the broken, maimed crucifix, examined by the angry Town Priest. He begins to pray.
EXT. THE HILL

-Geppetto in the WINTER, cleaning Carlo's grave. -Geppetto in the SPRING, laying flowers on the grave. -Geppetto in the SUMMER, telling stories with puppets. -Geppetto in the FALL, singing a song with his accordion.
INT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP - THE YEARS PASS.
The years passed, the world moved on, but Geppetto did not...
Geppetto DRINKS alone in his cold, lifeless bedroom. He stares at Carlo's old SCHOOL BOOK and a few other mementos. He passes out, asleep.
SPRING -- Saturday, APRIL 9, 1939.
A PINE TREE has grown out of Carlo's grave. Storm clouds.
And this is where I come in...

CAMERA discovers SEBASTIAN J. CRICKET. A somewhat elegant cricket, but still a cricket. He hops along the grass, uphillcarrying a CANVAS BAG AND A SUITCASE in one arm, and a LEATHER-BOUND VOLUME AND A KNAPSACK tucked under the other.
Y'see I was a writer- and for years I'd been looking for the ideal conditions to set my illustrious, fascinating life-story to paper. Until at long last... I found it:
The Cricket approaches the grave and looks UP- To a nook in the pine tree. CRICKET (V.O.)
INT. NOOK - SUNSET
It begins to rain outside, but the nook is spacious and dry. The Cricket examines the space- standing on its own two hind legs- like a proud homeowner. He is making it "cozy".

HERE I could write my memoirs! And what a tale it would be! I had lived in a barrister's fireplace in Sardinia, sailed on the Adriatic in a fishing boat! Nested one Perugian winter with an acclaimed sculptor!! Cricket takes a portrait- places it on a knot in the nook and
then displays his inkwell and quill. He writes the title of his book:
"Stridulations of My Youth" By Sebastian J. Cricket. Then:
The Cricket looks out the nook.
EXT. THE HILL - SUNSET
Geppetto sprawled beside grave, bottle in hand in the rain.
(weeping) I dreamed of you, Carlo...I dreamed, you were right back here with me. My son. The Cricket watches Geppetto, crying by the grave. Oh dear.
If only I could have you back here... I'm so sorry...
I watched the old man weep and it moved me... And it turns out that I wasn't the only one watching him...
The Cricket looks into the woods. FROM THE WOODS THE WINGED FIGURE watches Geppetto.
In my many wanderings on this earth, I've learned that there are old spirits living in the mountains, in the forests...
The Cricket and her make eye contact.
Who rarely involve themselves in
the human worldThe GLOWING EYEBALLS fade into the bark of the trees.
But on occasion... They do.
I want you back, Carlo- right here...with me!
GEPPETTO SLAMS HIS FIST AGAINST THE TREE.
Why won't you listen to my prayers! Why!!
A deluge of rain pours down from the branches. The Cricket takes one last pitiful look at furious Geppetto, as he marches away, stumbling- shakes his head, and goes back inside.
INT. NOOK - NIGHT
Cricket sits down and starts to write againCRICKET
Perugia. when-His handwriting shakes- wavers. The whole nook shakes.
Whoaaah! What in my antennae-? Oooof!!!
EXT. THE HILL - NIGHT
From Cricket's POV inside the nook, we see Geppetto using an axe to chop down the tree. Lighting! THUNDER!
INT. NOOK - SAME
The Cricket's world SHAKES with every hack of the axe, causing him to lose balance. He scrambles to keep his papers safe.
I will have him back. I'll make Carlo again.
Wuhh...Hey!!
Out of this accursed pine!!
EXT. THE HILL - NIGHT
Rain pours down. Wind whirls. Lightning! THUNDER CRACKS! Geppetto hacks the tree with an axe.
INT. NOOK - NIGHT
C-c-crack! Tree falls. Cricket goes weightless (ref: Magnolia ambulance scene).
It lands HARD. His papers strewn all over. EXT. THE HILL Geppetto drags a piece of the trunk back towards-
EXT. GEPPETTO HOUSE
-His house.
(Gasps)
INT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP

The Cricket pops out of the tree like a submarine captain, dizzy from his journey. He is inside the workshop. He follows Geppetto in his drinking binge: stumbling drunk and retrieving sharp carving instruments. SHINK! A bark spud slides across surface, almost decapitating Cricket!
What on earth? Nooo!

He narrowly escapes and hides in a cuckoo clock. As Cricket watches with morbid fascination, camera adjusts to reveal a TINY MAN standing right behind him! Wait, no, it's just a wooden peg doll from the cuckoo clock. Phew! HE almost kills the Cricket as the clock strikes the hour.
Oww! Ohhh! Oooh!
Geppetto looks at a picture of Carlo (one ear visible in photo). He witnesses Geppetto carve Pinocchio- furiously. Vehemently.
It's a house of horrors! (gasps and covers his eyes)

He finishes one beautifully carved ear, little curls of hair on one side of his head. He touches the ear tenderly. He cries at the sight. He prays- to no one in particular. Geppetto hammers some nails. Attaches a carved hand. Hinges a knee. Places the head on the neck joint. We see an unfinished, basic puppet, sketched but crude. PINOCCHIO.
I... I will finish you tomorrow...
Geppetto stumbles to the stairs.
Yes, tomorrow...

But passes out halfway up the steps- slides down and slumps on the floor. A glowing eye blinks into view in front of Geppetto. It inspects him. Cricket watches curiously. Another eye floats in to inspect Cricket.
Shoo, Shoo, Shoo, Shoo, Shoo! Cricket sees eyes are swarming all around Pinocchio.
Hey! Get away from there! Go away! Bugger off!
Cricket jumps to Pinocchio, trying to scare away the eyes.
No, no, no, no, no! This is my home! No trespassing! Go, go, go, go. Get away from here! Go! Go away! Go away!
Eyes drift away.
Yes, that's right!

The eyes begin to form a FIGURE. The FIGURE DISPLAYS its wings (covered with eyes in place of feathers) and reveals the benign face of a PALE WOOD SPRITE. The face has clear similarities to Carlo's mother's face, but is simplified and statuesque.
The Cricket watches as the Pale Figure approaches Pinocchio.
(in awe) Whoa!
Little wooden boy... May you rise with the sun and walk the earth...
Excuse me- Can I help you?
Unnervingly, the FIGURE half turns, ALL EYES ON THE CRICKET, WINGS FLUTTERING.
This is my home we're talking about!
He hops towards the Wood Sprite.
(harrumphs) May I ask- Who on earth are you?
On Earth? A Guardian. I care for the little things, the forgotten things- the lost ones.
Well, I am Sebastian J. Cricket: HOMEOWNER. And I have every right to be consulted about your schemes and machinations regarding my property.
Well, since you already live in the heart of the wooden boy, perhaps you can help me.
Help you what?
To watch over him: guide him to be good.
I'm not a governess, madam, I am a novelist- a raconteur- currently immersed in writing my memoirs.
Well, in this world you get what you give. Take on this responsibility, and I will grant you one wish.
And that could be anything? Anything at all? Cricket scratches his antenna with his hind leg.
The publication of my book? Fame? Fortune? Anything.
WOOD SPRITE
Cricket looks to Pinocchio, who lays in the corner, half in shadow, limbs akimbo.
Hmm, maybe I CAN help. I'll try my best and that's the best anyone can do! (beat) Hehe, that's rather clever, isn't it?
Cricket turns back to Wood Sprite, who touches the puppet with her GLOWING RIGHT HAND: WOOD SPRITE Little wooden boy. Made of pine- We

son- fill his days with light- so he'll never be alone. The Cricket is perched on Pinocchio's nose. ZAPPP! He is electrocuted by the Wood Sprite's magic. He falls to the table. She then fades away... The light fades away as well.
INT. FIREPLACE - MORNING

Geppetto wakes up at the bottom of the stairs. Through the fog of sleep we hear faint footsteps and clattering. He looks around, hungover but curious of the sounds. He notices: The Puppet is gone. He notices: The picture of Carlo, knocked over and covered in wood shavings. He looks under the workshop table: Nothing. He hears more noise. Reacts quickly and bumps his head on the table. Clattering. It's coming from above. A ladder leads to an attic crawlspace over the workshop.
Who goes there??
Geppetto grabs an AXE from the ground and moves cautiously.
I...I'm warning you. I...I have a weapon!
He climbs the ladder, and enters the attic.
INT. ATTIC - MORNING

Suspenseful horror movie sequence: Geppetto pops his head into the dim attic. In the dark corner is a strange, unnatural writhing-andrattling THING. Geppetto approaches trepidatiously. As his eyes adjust he sees there is something ALIVE in the attic. The THING steps into the light like Frankenstein, revealing its grotesquely lit face: it is PINOCCHIO!
Good morning, Papa! Pinocchio is alive!
ARRRRRGH!!!
Geppetto screams in absolute horror!!
W-what is this??! What kind of sorcery?!
You wanted me to live! You asked for me to live!
Who- Who--are you!??
My name is Pinocchio! I'm your son!
Geppetto frantically holds the axe.
You're not my son! Don't come near me!
Sebastian J. pops out of Pinocchio's chest.
The boy is telling the truth,
Arrrgh!! It's full of cockroaches!
Geppetto recoils. The old man backs up in terror, hitting his head on the low ceiling, tripping backwards through the crawlspace door, hitting his chin as he falls down...
INT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP - MORNING

...and landing on the hard floor of the workshop. The axe tumbles from his hand. Pinocchio descends the ladder with disjointed, inhuman movements; he's not yet in full control of his body (ref: a baby's weird twitchy movements / floppy wooden puppet). Geppetto, flat on his back, tries to pull himself further away from this monstrosity. Pinocchio starts to look around the room in wonder. He picks up the axe and looks at it with curiosity. He gasps and runs off, giggling, as he sees something else of interest. Having lost interest in the axe, he tosses it aside. Luckily this time Geppetto is able to catch it before it strikes him.
(gasps) Wow...What is this?
Another gasp, as Pinocchio sees a row of glinting empty bottles. He rushes over.
All the things my eyes can see...
No...No. Get away from me!
Everything is new to me.
You, you stand back! Don't come near me!

segment-speaker: PINOCCHIO Yo dee lo dee lee
segment-text: What do you call it, call it?
It...It's a clock. Don't touch it!
What to do with it, with it?
It sings at 6 o'clock.
Pinocchio hops around the room causing destruction in his glee.
PINOCCHIO (SONG)
Yo dee lo dee lo yo dee
Oh no! Argh!
Pinocchio begins to pick up objects he sees around the room.
Yo dee lo dee lo yo lee What do you call it, call it?
No, no, please!
Geppetto chases him, trying to calm him- picking up after him. Sebastian stays inside the nook- holds on for dear life.
That's a hammer.
What to do with it, with it?
You tap, you smash, you shatter.
Love it. I love it.
Yo dee lo dee lo yo dee lo dee lo
Pinocchio smashes with the hammer, shattering all the
Oh no! No! Ugh!
Everything is new to me, To me
Ca-Careful!

Pinocchio grabs a broom and swings it around, dancing with it like Fred Astaire. Cricket, inside the nook, hangs on for dear life. Pinocchio climbs up and swings from the ceiling. In his enthusiasm, he knocks some knives off a shelf. They come dangerously close to stabbing Geppetto, but he manages to leap out of the way as they pierce the ground all around him.
Ahh, sharp! Sharp!! Ahhh!

The world is rich with funny words They ring like bells those tiny words They glow, they shine, they dance in my mind Like a chorus line, those silly words of mine La la eee dooo La la eee dooo What do you call it, call it?
Uhh, a--a chamberpot.
Pinocchio begins running around again clutching the bedpan.
What to do with it, with it?
Um-uh I...oh.
Love it I love it
Yo dee lo dee lo yo dee lo dee lo
Everything is new Everything is new to me
Yo dee lo dee lo yo dee Yo dee lo dee lo yo dee
Oh dear, oh dear. Well, alright, alright, that's enough. That's enough. Ohhh. Don't. Don't! No! No! You have to stop!
Everything is new to me!

Geppetto stares disgusted at his unholy creation. Geppetto throws a blanket over Pinocchio- yodelling. Then nervously runs and pushes Pinocchio- blanket and allinto a closet. Closes the closet door. Locks it. Pinocchio BANGS loudly, trying to escape.
That was so much fun, Papa!
You're not my son! (beat) What is wrong with you!?
It's quiet. He stares at the door apprehensively. I'm sorry.

Geppetto opens the door slowly to look inside. Pinocchio sits in the corner. A glove is stuck on the end of his nose. He makes for a ridiculous figure, and for a moment, Geppetto's heart begins to soften.
I--uh... (searching for the words) Just stay here- don't come out
The CHURCH BELLS ring. Backs away from the door.
GEPPETTO (CONT'D)
Time for ChurchTrying to pretend like it's a normal day, he heads to front door.
Church?! I wanna go to Church!! Church!! Church!! Church!!
Stay there. You- you understand?
Opens the front door, and closes roughly behind him. Pinocchio continues to bang on the locked closet door.
Church!! Church!! Church!! Church!
SMASH! Pinocchio BURSTS out of the closet- yelling!! Church!!!
No no. He told you to stayPINOCCHIO (sing song) I'm going to church, I'm going to church, I'm going to church!
Pinocchio continues to head towards the door.
No, no, no! Please stop, you must obey your Papa. "Obey"?
To do as you are told...
But I don't want to obey.
Well, you must try your best, and that's the best anybody can do.
MUSIC SWELLS as if in the start of a musical number. Cricket sings oh, ever so briefly with a perfect baritone voice...
My dear father loved to say--Pinocchio opens the door, slamming it into the Cricket, crushing him and cutting off his song.
I'm going to church!! I'm going to church!!
Pinocchio opens the door and leaves. Ugggh.
EXT. TOWN STREETS AND PIAZZA - DAY
The town has changed. POSTERS OF FASCIST PROPAGANDA are everywhere. Portraits of Mussolini take entire walls. Pinocchio goes by one that reads: BELIEVE, OBEY, FIGHT. He waves to the mural.
Hiiiii! Okay, byeeeee! Some houses are in disrepair- destroyed, abandoned.
Pinocchio-hears the bell and seesTHE TOWN'S CHURCH. Oooh!
Pinocchio makes some playful noises as he proceeds to hop up each church step.
INT. CHURCH
The Town Priest prepares to bless palms with holy water and incense. Everybody is singing.
Ostende nobis, Domine, misericordiam Tuam. CONGREGATION Et salutare Tuum da nobis.
Pater omnipotens, aeterne Deus. CONGREGATION
Et clamor meus ad Te veniat.
Pater omnipotens, aeterne Deus.

Everyone closes their eyes and clasps hands in prayer. Pinocchio watches with wonder. Without realizing it, he wanders down the aisle. Pinocchio looks at the Christ, backlit by a sun-ray, majestic... missing an arm. He catches the eye of CANDLEWICK.
Look- Father- over there- what is that??
Someone in the crowd screams.
Ahh! An abhorration.
Papa! Papa! Over here!
It speaks!?
Pinocchio!
(runs to Geppetto) Papa! It's me! I came to church!
The crowd lets out a collective gasp.
CARNIVAL WORKER #1
Witchcraft!
WOMAN 2 makes the gesture of the horns to protect herself from Malocchio, the "evil eye"!
Malocchio!!
(correcting her) Pinocchio!
People are disgusted and terrified, making sign of the cross on themselves.
No!! No!! Please. It's...It's a puppet- To- to entertain!! Everyone chatters/gossips/argues with each other.
If he's a puppet, where are his strings?
The Podesta calmly stands and begins to walk down the aisle. The crowd continues to murmur.
That's true. Who controls you, wooden boy?
Of course, I control him.
Who controls YOU?
Geppetto picks him up and covers his mouth- but Pinocchio struggles to escape.
(being kicked and hit by Pinocchio) (MORE)
No one talks to the Podesta like that. (awkwardly chuckling) He- oh, he's a puppet, just a puppet.
No, I'm not! I'm made of flesh and bone and meaty bits!! I'm a real boy! His nose grows! People scream again!
Arrrrgh!!
TWIN DAUGHTER 2
A monster!!! OLD WOMAN
No, no...no! He's harmless!!
It's an abomination!
(Shouting in Italian) TWIN DAUGHTER 1 This is the work of the Devil!
ENOUGH! This is a house of God. You drunken fool! You carving this, this...thing! While our blessed Christ hangs unfinished all these years! Take that unholy thing away! Take it away! Now!
(Shouting in Italian)
Yes, yes, Padre. Uh-uh-uh sorry, sorry, sorry, sorry. He'll be fine.
Geppetto picks Pinocchio up and tries to leave the church!
Shame on you, Geppetto! Burn him!
Chop 'im up!
Oh mio dio! (continues cursing in Italian) Candlewick sticks his tongue out at Pinocchio and Pinocchio responds in kind. Get out!!
OLD WOMAN
Curse you, Geppetto!
The lord will punish him! OLD WOMAN (yelling in Italian) Get out!!!
TWIN DAUGHTER 1
The church doors slam behind them.
INT. GEPPETTO WORKSHOP - AFTERNOON
It is raining outside. Geppetto is filing off the excess nose from Pinocchio. Pinocchio is fidgeting. And yodeling.
Yo dee lo dee lo yo lee. Hello!
Stop fidgeting, please! (he doesn't)
Cricket watches from a ledge while tuning a tiny fiddle. He waves at Pinocchio.
Carlo never acted like this. (sighs)
Papa- Why did my nose grow today?
You lied, Pinocchio. And a lie isplain to see as your nose... And...
And the more you lie, the more it grows... Is that it?
Pinocchio extends his finger out from his nose to illustrate. Geppetto is startled- how does Pinocchio know that?? That-
yes- that's it-

A KNOCK on the door. Geppetto goes to answer. Lightning illuminates: The Town Priest and The Podesta (in Fascist Regalia). More lightning illuminates: Candlewick- Glaring menacingly. The Podesta gives a ROMAN SALUTE! They all do.
INT. GEPPETTO HOUSE / DINING ROOM - NIGHT
Geppetto pours his visitors some hot chocolate.
There you go.
